raw_consumer_statement:
I am writing to file a formal complaint against Barclays Bank Delaware regarding its handling of my fraud dispute ( Account ending in XXXX ). 

On XX/XX/year>, I received a letter from Barclays ( copy attached ) stating that after a thorough investigation, they had denied my fraud claim, concluding that the charges were made by me or someone with authority to use my account. Barclays has therefore held me responsible for the previously disputed balance, along with finance charges and fees. 

I strongly dispute this conclusion for the following reasons : Lack of Sufficient Evidence Barclays has not provided any substantiating documentation ( e.g., transaction verification, merchant evidence, or proof of authorization ) that supports their determination that I authorized or benefited from these charges. 

Failure to Properly Investigate Simply concluding that the charges were made by me or someone with authority is inadequate without demonstrating how such a conclusion was reached. No details of the investigation, merchant records, or other supporting information were shared with me. 

Violation of Consumer Protections Under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ) and Regulation Z, issuers are required to conduct a reasonable investigation of disputed charges. I believe Barclays response fails to meet this standard, as they have not proven the legitimacy of the disputed transactions. 

As a consumer, I should not be held liable for fraudulent transactions unless the issuer can provide clear and convincing evidence that I authorized them. Barclays has not met this burden.
